message:
test(conformance): add window parity matrix

Adds live pandas oracle coverage for Series rolling, expanding, EWM, and DataFrame rolling mean edge cases. Closes frankenpandas-trc6. Adds review bead frankenpandas-vx91 for rolling-count null-window drift.

files.patch:
@@ -593,7 +593,7 @@
 {"id":"frankenpandas-tlkc","title":"Add series str.isdecimal unicode-ascii strict conformance packets","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-19T01:47:49.460237646Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T01:51:19.528241535Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T01:51:19.526461365Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-tne4","title":"[HIGH] semver: zero #[non_exhaustive] on any public enum (incl. 5 error enums) — every new variant after 0.1.0 is a semver break","description":"Semver-stability audit on public error enums.

**Observation.** grep for `#[non_exhaustive]` across all 12 crates: **zero matches**. Meanwhile, the public error-enum surface includes:

  fp-expr/src/lib.rs:142          pub enum ExprError
  fp-index/src/lib.rs:1267        pub enum IndexError
  fp-io/src/lib.rs:23             pub enum IoError
  fp-conformance/src/lib.rs:2890  pub enum HarnessError
  fp-frame/src/lib.rs:26          pub enum FrameError

plus these crates' total public-surface type counts:
  fp-types:    7 pub enum|struct
  fp-expr:     6 pub enum|struct
  fp-io:       11 pub enum|struct
  fp-frame:    35 pub enum|struct

(12-crate total substantially higher — these four are samples.)

**Semver implication.**
- Per Rust's semver policy (codified by cargo-semver-checks + https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/reference/semver.html), adding a new variant to a `pub enum` without `#[non_exhaustive]` is a MAJOR version break.
- Downstream users who `match` on the enum with an exhaustive pattern compile-break when the enum grows. Users who match with `_ => ...` don't break, but not all users do.
- Error enums in an active library grow constantly: new IO formats → new IoError variants, new expr syntax → new ExprError variants, etc. Our swarm's velocity makes additions almost guaranteed over any multi-week window.
- Without `#[non_exhaustive]`, every single variant addition after v0.1.0 forces bumping to v0.2.0 — rapidly, with breaking implications for anyone pinned to 0.1.x.

**Concrete upcoming breaks:**
- br-frankenpandas-fd90 (SQL backend epic) will add variants to IoError for PostgreSQL/MySQL backend errors. Without #[non_exhaustive]: semver break.
- br-frankenpandas-1zzp (row MultiIndex, closed) already added variants to FrameError for row-MI validation. If 0.1.0 had shipped before, 1zzp would have forced 0.2.0.
- br-frankenpandas-niwb (error-path conformance) will add ~20 variants across IoError + FrameError + ExprError to carry pandas-matched category tags. Without #[non_exhaustive]: semver break per family.
- br-frankenpandas-7cfm (#Panics: contract) might promote some panics to Err(SomeVariant); each new variant → break.

**Severity.** HIGH — semver-compliance gap that is almost free to fix today (add one attribute per error enum) but expensive to retrofit after 0.1.0 ships. br-4clx (release workflow) is the deadline: whatever non-exhaustive story ships in 0.1.0 is the contract we're stuck with.

**What `#[non_exhaustive]` does.**
```rust
#[non_exhaustive]
pub enum IoError {
    Parse(String),
    Io(io::Error),
    // ... existing variants ...
}
```
Downstream users matching on IoError:
```rust
match err {
    IoError::Parse(s) => ...,
    IoError::Io(e) => ...,
    // Forced: must have a _ arm because enum is non_exhaustive.
    _ => panic!("unknown IoError variant"),
}
```
The `_` arm is the seat belt that lets us add Parse2 / Io2 / NewVariant without breaking downstream.

**Fix shape:**

1. Add `#[non_exhaustive]` to EVERY public enum in fp-* crates where:
   - The enum is a pub error type. (Mandatory.)
   - The enum represents a config option, opt-set, kind-tag, or anything that might grow. (Recommended — add unless the enum is genuinely closed, like an enum of 4 known states that can never be extended.)

2. Audit pub structs too. `#[non_exhaustive]` on structs prevents construction-by-literal from outside the crate but allows .field access. Apply to config structs (CsvReadOptions, ExcelReadOptions, ExcelWriteOptions, JsonOrient variants, etc.) where new fields are likely. Don't apply to small value-type structs (Scalar, IndexLabel) that users might legitimately construct via literal.

3. CI gate: add `cargo-semver-checks` to CI (as its own job, cheap). On every PR, semver-checks compares the crate's public API vs the last published version (once br-4clx releases). Flags breaking changes before they ship.

4. Document in CONTRIBUTING.md: "all new pub enums start with `#[non_exhaustive]`; remove the attribute only when the enum's variants are provably closed and stable."

5. README capability-table or a new SEMVER.md: explain the `#[non_exhaustive]` policy so downstream users know why they need a `_` match arm.

Blast radius:
- ~15-25 enum annotations across the workspace (~30 lines total).
- ~20-40 struct annotations if we also apply to config structs.
- 1 new CI job (cargo-semver-checks install + run, ~15 lines in ci.yml).
- CONTRIBUTING.md + SEMVER.md additions (~30 lines).

Zero source-logic changes. Attribute-only diff + CI wiring.

Prerequisites:
- None to start. Attribute additions can land today.
- br-4clx (release workflow) — first release MUST have the correct non_exhaustive story; coordinate timing.
- br-ffhs (CI job splitting) — cargo-semver-checks becomes the 6th parallel CI job in the refactor.

**Related:**
- br-4clx (release + semver policy) — this bead operationalizes the policy.
- br-niwb (error-path conformance) — error-variant adds naturally happen in niwb's PRs; non_exhaustive prevents those from being semver breaks.
- br-fd90 (SQL backend epic) — slice 1's SqlConnection trait (landed) is the one non_exhaustive attribute that MIGHT already exist; the rest needs audit.
- br-7cfm (#Panics: docs) — a pub fn that previously panicked now returning Err adds a variant; non_exhaustive makes that non-breaking.
- br-liai (differential fuzz) — when a fuzz-discovered bug triggers "add a specific Err variant to reject this input," non_exhaustive means the fix is a minor version bump not a major.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T21:34:03.744321423Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-23T16:28:02.335005298Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T16:28:02.332465986Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api-stability","release","review","semver"]}

+{"id":"frankenpandas-vx91","title":"[HIGH] REVIEW: Series rolling count null windows diverge from pandas","description":"Window conformance matrix frankenpandas-trc6 found a live pandas divergence. Failing input: operation=series_rolling_count, window_size=2, left series name=value, index=[utf8 dup, utf8 dup, utf8 tail], values=[float64 1.0, null null, float64 3.0]. Expected from pandas Series([1.0, None, 3.0], index=['dup','dup','tail']).rolling(2).count(): [NaN, 1.0, 1.0]. Actual FrankenPandas drift records: series.values[1] actual=Null(NaN) expected=Float64(1.0); series.values[2] actual=Null(NaN) expected=Float64(1.0). Severity HIGH because rolling count null/min_periods semantics are pandas-observable window parity.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T07:00:39.799564383Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T07:00:39.799564383Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -825,6 +825,158 @@ def op_series_combine_first(pd, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
     return {"expected_series": series_to_expected(out)}
 
 
+def resolve_window_size(payload: dict[str, Any], op_name: str) -> int:
+    raw = payload.get("window_size", 3)
+    if not isinstance(raw, int) or isinstance(raw, bool) or raw <= 0:
+        raise OracleError(f"{op_name} requires positive integer window_size")
+    return raw
+
+
+def resolve_min_periods(payload: dict[str, Any], op_name: str) -> int | None:
+    raw = payload.get("min_periods")
+    if raw is None:
+        return None
+    if not isinstance(raw, int) or isinstance(raw, bool) or raw < 0:
+        raise OracleError(f"{op_name} requires non-negative integer min_periods")
+    return raw
+
+
+def op_series_rolling_builtin(
+    pd, payload: dict[str, Any], func: str, op_name: str
+) -> dict[str, Any]:
+    series = fixture_series_from_payload(pd, payload.get("left"), op_name)
+    window_size = resolve_window_size(payload, op_name)
+    min_periods = resolve_min_periods(payload, op_name)
+    center = bool(payload.get("window_center", False))
+    try:
+        out = getattr(
+            series.rolling(window=window_size, min_periods=min_periods, center=center),
+            func,
+        )()
+    except Exception as exc:
+        raise OracleError(f"{op_name} failed: {exc}") from exc
+    return {"expected_series": series_to_expected(out)}
+
+
+def op_series_rolling_mean(pd, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
+    return op_series_rolling_builtin(pd, payload, "mean", "series_rolling_mean")
+
+
+def op_series_rolling_sum(pd, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
+    return op_series_rolling_builtin(pd, payload, "sum", "series_rolling_sum")
+
+
+def op_series_rolling_std(pd, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
+    return op_series_rolling_builtin(pd, payload, "std", "series_rolling_std")
+
+
+def op_series_rolling_min(pd, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
+    return op_series_rolling_builtin(pd, payload, "min", "series_rolling_min")
+
+
+def op_series_rolling_max(pd, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
+    return op_series_rolling_builtin(pd, payload, "max", "series_rolling_max")
+
+
+def op_series_rolling_var(pd, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
+    return op_series_rolling_builtin(pd, payload, "var", "series_rolling_var")
+
+
+def op_series_rolling_count(pd, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
+    return op_series_rolling_builtin(pd, payload, "count", "series_rolling_count")
+
+
+def op_series_expanding_builtin(
+    pd, payload: dict[str, Any], func: str, op_name: str
+) -> dict[str, Any]:
+    series = fixture_series_from_payload(pd, payload.get("left"), op_name)
+    min_periods = resolve_min_periods(payload, op_name)
+    try:
+        out = getattr(series.expanding(min_periods=min_periods), func)()
+    except Exception as exc:
+        raise OracleError(f"{op_name} failed: {exc}") from exc
+    return {"expected_series": series_to_expected(out)}
+
+
+def op_series_expanding_count(pd, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
+    return op_series_expanding_builtin(pd, payload, "count", "series_expanding_count")
+
+
+def op_series_expanding_sum(pd, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
+    return op_series_expanding_builtin(pd, payload, "sum", "series_expanding_sum")
+
+
+def op_series_expanding_mean(pd, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
+    return op_series_expanding_builtin(pd, payload, "mean", "series_expanding_mean")
+
+
+def op_series_expanding_min(pd, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
+    return op_series_expanding_builtin(pd, payload, "min", "series_expanding_min")
+
+
+def op_series_expanding_max(pd, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
+    return op_series_expanding_builtin(pd, payload, "max", "series_expanding_max")
+
+
+def op_series_expanding_std(pd, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
+    return op_series_expanding_builtin(pd, payload, "std", "series_expanding_std")
+
+
+def op_series_expanding_var(pd, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
+    return op_series_expanding_builtin(pd, payload, "var", "series_expanding_var")
+
+
+def op_series_expanding_quantile(pd, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
+    series = fixture_series_from_payload(pd, payload.get("left"), "series_expanding_quantile")
+    min_periods = resolve_min_periods(payload, "series_expanding_quantile")
+    q = payload.get("quantile_value", 0.5)
+    if not isinstance(q, (int, float)) or isinstance(q, bool) or q < 0.0 or q > 1.0:
+        raise OracleError("series_expanding_quantile requires 0.0 <= quantile_value <= 1.0")
+    try:
+        out = series.expanding(min_periods=min_periods).quantile(float(q))
+    except Exception as exc:
+        raise OracleError(f"series_expanding_quantile failed: {exc}") from exc
+    return {"expected_series": series_to_expected(out)}
+
+
+def op_series_ewm_mean(pd, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
+    series = fixture_series_from_payload(pd, payload.get("left"), "series_ewm_mean")
+    span = payload.get("ewm_span")
+    alpha = payload.get("ewm_alpha")
+    if span is None and alpha is None:
+        span = 10.0
+    if span is not None and (not isinstance(span, (int, float)) or span <= 1.0):
+        raise OracleError("series_ewm_mean requires ewm_span > 1")
+    if alpha is not None and (
+        not isinstance(alpha, (int, float)) or alpha <= 0.0 or alpha > 1.0
+    ):
+        raise OracleError("series_ewm_mean requires 0.0 < ewm_alpha <= 1.0")
+    try:
+        out = series.ewm(
+            span=None if span is None else float(span),
+            alpha=None if alpha is None else float(alpha),
+            adjust=False,
+            ignore_na=True,
+        ).mean()
+    except Exception as exc:
+        raise OracleError(f"series_ewm_mean failed: {exc}") from exc
+    return {"expected_series": series_to_expected(out)}
+
+
+def op_dataframe_rolling_mean(pd, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
+    frame_payload = payload.get("frame")
+    if frame_payload is None:
+        raise OracleError("dataframe_rolling_mean requires frame payload")
+    window_size = resolve_window_size(payload, "dataframe_rolling_mean")
+    min_periods = resolve_min_periods(payload, "dataframe_rolling_mean")
+    frame = dataframe_from_json(pd, frame_payload)
+    try:
+        out = frame.rolling(window=window_size, min_periods=min_periods).mean()
+    except Exception as exc:
+        raise OracleError(f"dataframe_rolling_mean failed: {exc}") from exc
+    return {"expected_frame": dataframe_to_json(out)}
+
+
 def op_series_asof(pd, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
     left = payload.get("left")
     if left is None:
@@ -4891,6 +5043,38 @@ def dispatch(pd, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
         return op_series_extract_df(pd, payload)
     if op == "series_extractall":
         return op_series_extractall(pd, payload)
+    if op == "series_rolling_mean":
+        return op_series_rolling_mean(pd, payload)
+    if op == "series_rolling_sum":
+        return op_series_rolling_sum(pd, payload)
+    if op == "series_rolling_std":
+        return op_series_rolling_std(pd, payload)
+    if op == "series_rolling_min":
+        return op_series_rolling_min(pd, payload)
+    if op == "series_rolling_max":

+    if op == "series_rolling_var":
+        return op_series_rolling_var(pd, payload)
+    if op == "series_rolling_count":

+        return op_series_expanding_sum(pd, payload)
+    if op == "series_expanding_mean":
+        return op_series_expanding_mean(pd, payload)
+    if op == "series_expanding_min":
+        return op_series_expanding_min(pd, payload)
+    if op == "series_expanding_max":
+        return op_series_expanding_max(pd, payload)
+    if op == "series_expanding_std":
+        return op_series_expanding_std(pd, payload)
+    if op == "series_expanding_var":
+        return op_series_expanding_var(pd, payload)
+    if op == "series_ewm_mean":
+        return op_series_ewm_mean(pd, payload)
     if op in {"dataframe_identity", "data_frame_identity"}:
         return op_dataframe_identity(pd, payload)
     if op in {"dataframe_to_json_records", "data_frame_to_json_records"}:
@@ -4953,6 +5137,8 @@ def dispatch(pd, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
         return op_dataframe_groupby_rolling_std(pd, payload)
     if op in {"dataframe_groupby_rolling_var", "data_frame_groupby_rolling_var"}:
         return op_dataframe_groupby_rolling_var(pd, payload)
+    if op in {"dataframe_rolling_mean", "data_frame_rolling_mean"}:
+        return op_dataframe_rolling_mean(pd, payload)
     if op in {"dataframe_groupby_cumcount", "data_frame_groupby_cumcount"}:
         return op_dataframe_groupby_cumcount(pd, payload)
     if op in {"dataframe_groupby_ngroup", "data_frame_groupby_ngroup"}:
